title: Tennis Players Live Nearly a Decade Longer. The Reason Has Nothing to Do With Fitness.
description: A 25-year Danish cohort study found tennis players outlive sedentary people by 9.7 years — more than runners, cyclists, or swimmers. The leading explanation isn't cardiovascular fitness. It's that tennis is structurally impossible to play alone, and social isolation kills. Three independent datasets, one uncomfortable conclusion.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Tennis Players Live Nearly a Decade Longer. The Reason Has Nothing to Do With Fitness.
